item.title: [Bloomberg][Markets] Scottie Pippen Helps Sell Wall Street on Prediction Markets
item.description: At a finance conference in Boca Raton, Florida, when Wall Street traders weren't shooting hoops with Scottie Pippen, they were talking about prediction markets and what they might mean for the industry (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: [Bloomberg][Geopolitics] Trump Hopes China and Other Nations Will Send Ships to Hormuz
item.description: “Hopefully China, France, Japan, South Korea, the UK, and others, that are affected by this artificial constraint, will send Ships,” President Donald Trump says in a Truth Social post.

item.title: [Bloomberg][General] Malaysia Graft Chief Alleged to Hold Millions in Shares, MP Says
item.description: A Malaysian lawmaker said a probe into the shareholdings of the country’s anti-graft chief Azam Baki allegedly uncovered that he owned shares worth 14 million ringgit ($3.6 million) in nine companies.

item.title: [FT][Geopolitics] UAE port struck after US hits military assets at Iranian export hub
item.description: Authorities say Fujairah attack was intercepted and blaze under control after American forces target Kharg Island

item.title: [Bloomberg][General] Why Sweden Is Becoming a Defense Powerhouse as Europe Rearms
item.description: Sweden is known for its social welfare system and has a long history of neutrality, but as Europe races to rebuild its military strength after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the Nordic country is emerging as an important player. We take a look at how Saab and Sweden’s broader defense ecosystem have become central to Europe’s rearmament, why the country was better prepared than many of its neighbors, and what its European peers can learn from it. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: [Bloomberg][General] Thames Water Creditors Pitch Improved Rescue Offer to Ofwat
item.description: Thames Water’s creditors have pitched an improved rescue offer for the troubled utility that includes a bigger equity injection and an increase in committed debt, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: [Bloomberg][Geopolitics] Coming up on The Weekend Show | Strikes on Iran Continue
item.description: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity and context to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: [Bloomberg][AI] HORMUZ TRACKER: Saudi Suezmax Reappears Off Mumbai; LPG to India
item.description: Commercial vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continues at very low levels, with just a handful of oil and liquefied petroleum gas tankers transiting in the last 24 hours amid heightened regional tensions.

item.title: [Bloomberg][Energy] Second Dynacom Oil Tanker Passes Hormuz as Other Owners Hold Back
item.description: A Greek shipowner sent a second oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, bucking caution among the shipping industry as Iran lashes out across the region in response to attacks by Israel and the US.

item.title: [Bloomberg][Geopolitics] Iran War Supercharges Secretive Korean Tycoon’s Big Tanker Bet
item.description: As the war in Iran wreaks chaos across global energy markets, one enigmatic Korean tycoon has seen his earnings soar.

item.title: [FT][General] Where are the Houthis?
item.description: Yemeni militant group is noticeably absent from Iran’s war with the US and Israel

item.title: [Bloomberg][AI] Two LPG Ships Sail Through Hormuz to Shortage-Hit India
item.description: Two tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas are heading to India after crossing the Strait of Hormuz, providing some relief to acute shortages as the war in the Persian Gulf disrupts supply of the cooking fuel.

item.title: [FT][AI] Sovereign AI is a bet on the economies of anti-scale
item.description: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers

item.title: [FT][Geopolitics] Why Iran could become the next Iraq
item.description: The first Gulf war left a weakened regime in place without a political settlement — paving the way for future conflict

item.title: [FT][Geopolitics] Are markets being too complacent about the Iran war?
item.description: Sometimes crises do not blow over as investors might hope

item.title: [FT][General] Donald Trump, globalist
item.description: It should not have taken the Iran war to finish the idea that he is ‘isolationist’

item.title: [FT][AI] Musk’s $134bn claim against OpenAI rests on ‘numbers out of the air’, judge says
item.description: California court questions billionaire’s expert witness but declines to exclude the testimony from April trial

item.title: [FT][AI] Nvidia prepares AI ‘inference’ chip launch to counter rising challengers
item.description: Jensen Huang to unveil new products at GTC event next week as spending shifts from training to running AI models

item.title: [FT][Energy] The oil price war
item.description: For an Iranian regime facing existential struggle, pushing crude up is key measure of success

item.title: [FT][AI] Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters
item.description: Tesla and SpaceX managers sent in to review work as billionaire’s start-up struggles to keep pace with rivals
